author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
In What Respects Predestination and Grace Differ.
title: In What Respects Predestination and Grace Differ.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Chapter 19 [X]—In What Respects Predestination and Grace Differ.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Moreover, that which I said, “That the salvation of this religion has never been lacking to him who was worthy of it, and that he to whom it was lacking was not worthy,”—if it be discussed and it be asked whence any man can be worthy, there are not wanting those who say—by human will.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: But we say, by divine grace or predestination.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Further, between grace and predestination there is only this difference, that predestination is the preparation for grace, while grace is the donation itself.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: When, therefore the apostle says, “Not of works, lest any man should boast.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For we are His workmanship, created in Christ Jesus in good works,” it is grace; but what follows—“which God hath prepared that we should walk in them”—is predestination, which cannot exist without foreknowledge, although foreknowledge may exist without predestination; because God foreknew by predestination those things which He was about to do, whence it was said, “He made those things that shall be.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Moreover, He is able to foreknow even those things which He does not Himself do,—as all sins whatever.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Because, although there are some which are in such wise sins as that they are also the penalties of sins, whence it is said, “God gave them over to a reprobate mind, to do those things which are not convenient,” it is not in such a case the sin that is God’s, but the judgment.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Therefore God’s predestination of good is, as I have said, the preparation of grace; which grace is the effect of that predestination.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Therefore when God promised to Abraham in his seed the faith of the nations, saying, “I have established thee a father of many nations,” whence the apostle says, “Therefore it is of faith, that the promise, according to grace, might be established to all the seed,” He promised not from the power of our will but from His own predestination.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For He promised what He Himself would do, not what men would do.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Because, although men do those good things which pertain to God’s worship, He Himself makes them to do what He has commanded; it is not they that cause Him to do what He has promised.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Otherwise the fulfilment of God’s promises would not be in the power of God, but in that of men; and thus what was promised by God to Abraham would be given to Abraham by men themselves.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Abraham, however, did not believe thus, but “he believed, giving glory to God, that what He promised He is able also to do.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: He does not say, “to foretell”—he does not say, “to foreknow;” for He can foretell and foreknow the doings of strangers also; but he says, “He is able also to do;” and thus he is speaking not of the doings of others, but of His own.
